## ![][image1]

# High-Ranking Content Creation Template \+ Outline 

## **Overview**

| Content Idea *What’s this post about?* |  |  |  |
| ----- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Content Idea Category** *Broader/umbrella topic (keyword group) \*if applicable* |  |  |  |
| **Couples Planning Stage:** *Highlight relevant stage(s)* | Stage One \- Pre-engagement Stage Two \- Ideas & Inspirations Stage Three \- Highest Priority Decisions Stage Four \- Executing the Vision Stage Five \- Filling in the Details |  |  |
| **Keywords** | Keyword | Volume | Competition |
| Primary Keyword: |  |  |  |
| Secondary Keyword(s): |  |  |  |
| **Cannibalization check** *Have I made a post about this same topic (or search intent) already? Does Google think I have? (Am I ranking for this keyword?)*  | ***Google search for indexed pages on this topic**Site:yoursite.com primary keyword Site:yoursite.com secondary keyword(s) [**Google Search Console**](https://search.google.com/search-console) \> Performance \> Search Results \+ New \> Query Type in keywordClick “pages” tab to see ranking pages for that query* ***Should I create a new page or post on this topic?*** **Yes,** a new page/post is needed on this topic **No,** I need to update/improve an existing post |  |  |
| **Related Pages on my site to Link TO or FROM** | Copy/paste URL’s of pages that are related or are on broader/narrower topics here |  |  |

## 

## **Search Intent Analysis**

*Finding searcher goals, problems, motivations, needs, and desires* 

*Spend some time researching and putting yourself into the shoes of someone searching “primary keyword” so you can create a content piece that will fully satisfy their search (help them the most).*

- [ ] ### **Google the primary keyword for this piece of content** 

- [ ] ### **Examine what comes up on the results page and ask yourself the following questions.**        **Record any ideas of what you could include in your piece of content to satisfy their search in the [Content Outline](#content-outline) below** 

      * Who might be googling this? Who are they?   
          
      * What’s their “why” behind searching this term? If you were in their shoes (i.e. if you typed in this search) what would you be hoping to accomplish?   
        * Which potential goals of the searchers do the results seem to serve?

        

      * What could have motivated them to make this google search? What made them take the time to type it in and ask google vs. just continuing to think about it on their own or looking for an answer somewhere else?

      * WHO might this person searching this be hoping to hear from? And why? Whose opinion would they trust most? And why? (whether or not that’s you)  
          
      * What planning stage might they be in? What else might be on their mind even if it’s not in their search term?  
          
      * What problems could this person be facing?  
          
      * What do I know as an expert that will help them the most?   
          
      * What did they not search but is pretty dang important that they know (in your opinion)?  
          
      * Whether or not you’re willing to give it to them for free…. what would make them the most satisfied to find? What would solve their problem best and quickest? What would reduce their stress & give them relief to find?

## **Competition Analysis**

*Finding opportunities to outrank your competition*

*Spend some time examining what content is already out there and ranking for this keyword topic so you can identify what it’ll take to rank on the first page (and outrank) your competition by creating better content than them (or a different/fresh/unique perspective or spin).* 

- [ ] ### **Google the primary keyword for this piece of content** 

- [ ] ### **Examine the top \~5-8 results on the first page. List the ranking pages/post URL’s here and check each one off as you analyze it**

      - [ ] URL 1  
      - [ ] URL 2  
      - [ ] URL 3

- [ ] ### **Ask yourself the following questions as you analyze the content that’s currently ranking on the first page**        **Record any ideas of what you could include in your piece of content to satisfy their search in the [Content Outline](#content-outline) below** 

      * What kind of websites are ranking—are they big companies, individuals, directories

      * What kind of content formats are ranking—are they blog posts or something else?  
        * Is that the best format for truly delivering the best value to the person searching this & helping them the most?

      * What’s the breadth & depth of the pages that are ranking for this search?  
        * Is the breadth & depth of the top pages truly delivering the best experience & value to the searcher?

        

      * How can you give them better information (and a better experience) than the results that already exist?  
        * How could you minimize fluff and maximize the amount of valuable information?  
          * How can you format and structure your post in a way that is easy to digest?

      * What annoys you about who is ranking or what’s being said in the first page of results?  
        * What do you wish people were being educated about instead? 

      * What’s not being talked about by others or what unique perspective or experience do you have that could make your content stand out?  
        * What are the opportunities or gaps in their content that you can fill?

        

      * What are common subtopics that are coming up that everyone else is covering (and if you don’t include, your content might not be thorough enough to rank)?

## ---

## **Content Outline** {#content-outline}

### **Meta Title** 

*This is the title that will be shown in google search results*

- [ ] Look closely again at the search intent(s) for this keyword. How can you make it clear what your content is specifically offering and how clicking on \*your\* content will give them what they’re really after? (Better than competing content). Remember to make it about the person searching (not you), and consider the entire audience searching this term.  
- [ ] Include your primary keyword (toward the beginning of the title)  
- [ ] Aim for 40-65 characters (\~10-13 words). Use positive, enticing language.  
- [ ] Use [Mangools SERP Simulator](https://mangools.com/free-seo-tools/serp-simulator) (free) to make yours stand out amongst the competition 

|  |
| :---- |

### **Meta Description**

*This is the description that will be shown in google search results (note: sometimes google modifies this)*

- [ ] Look closely again at the search intent(s) for the primary & secondary keywords. How can you make it clear what your content is specifically offering and how clicking on \*your\* content will give them what they’re really after? (Better than competing content). Remember to make it about the person searching and “what is in it for them” (not about you). Remember to consider the entire audience searching this term.  
- [ ] Aim for 140-160 characters. Use positive, enticing language. Include a CTA if relevant.  
- [ ] Naturally your primary keyword and secondary keyword(s) where possible  
- [ ] Use [Mangools SERP Simulator](https://mangools.com/free-seo-tools/serp-simulator) (free) to make yours stand out amongst the competition 

|   |
| :---- |

---

## **Blog Post Outline Blocks**

***Note:** Copy/paste and edit heading & subheading below and customize by adding as many body sections and subsections as necessary to fully address the search intent for your keyword*

**H1 Tip:** This is the title of your blog post and can be different than your Meta title, Include your primary keyword

**H2 Tips:** These headings are the the tiles of the subsections of the “body” of your blog post and might be different tips, steps, ideas, FAQ’s, topics, reasons, how-to’s, answering common questions, (who, when, where, how, why)—they should address the main related topics and search intents (answer the biggest questions/problems) you identified with your search intent research.  

**Writing Tip** \- Keep your writing simple and clear (think 8th grade reading level). You’ll get all kinds of people coming to your blog with different education levels and those for whom your language is not their primary language. 

---

## **H1 Title (Include primary keyword)**

Intro Copy. About 100 words. Be sure to include your primary keyword. State the problem your couples are experiencing. Include the two/three things from your objective that the couple are going to learn. How will these things help solve their problem? Why should they read this post?

### **H2 Section Heading**

Body copy related to this heading. Each section of body copy should be between three to five paragraphs long. Each paragraph should be no more than three sentences. Each sentence should be no longer than 25 words.

#### **H3 Subsection Heading (if applicable)** Body copy related to this subsection heading. 

#### **H3 Subsection Heading (if applicable)** Body copy related to this subsection heading.  

### **H2 Section Heading**

Body copy related to this heading. Each section of body copy should be between three to five paragraphs long. Each paragraph should be no more than three sentences. Each sentence should be no longer than 25 words.

#### **H3 Subsection Heading (if applicable)** Body copy related to this subsection heading. 

#### **H3 Subsection Heading (if applicable)** Body copy related to this subsection heading. 

### **H2 Section Heading**

Body copy related to this heading. Each section of body copy should be between three to five paragraphs long. Each paragraph should be no more than three sentences. Each sentence should be no longer than 25 words.

#### **H3 Subsection Heading (if applicable)** Body copy related to this heading

#### **H3 Subsection Heading (if applicable)** Body copy related to this heading 

### **H2 Conclusion Heading**

Conclusion copy. Wrap up the main points of your post in about 60 words. Include a CTA. Your conclusion should be no longer than two paragraphs long.

---

## **Content Final Check**

- [ ] ### **Relevancy & thoroughness check**

      Look closely through your headings and content and do another Google search of different parts of your primary keyword to see what google displays (to see what google “knows” or “thinks” should be included or is relevant to this topic).

      TIP: Remove the word “wedding” or “elopement” from the keyword—and look at the search results. 

      Also quickly google search any locations (i.e. cities, parks, countries etc.), things (i.e. wedding dress, arch, florals) or people that are important to your blog content and see what google displays.

      Are there any additional ideas or facets about the locations/things/people that google brings up in its results that would be valuable for you to answer in your blog post?  

      Remember, google is trying to answer the questions most asked about each of these locations/things/people so if, so ensuring you’re completely covering a topic, check and make sure it isn’t missing anything obvious that you could add to your blog post to make it more valuable to users and relevant in google’s eyes.

- [ ] **Keyword Optimization Check**  
        
      Have you naturally included the most common phrases & terms that came up in your research? 

## **Optimization & Publishing Checklist**

- [ ] ### **Read Your Post Out Loud & Proofread**

      This is one of the best ways to check for errors and make sure it sounds friendly and conversational (like you would talk to a real client). Check out the Grammarly browser extension if you want help checking your grammar.

- [ ] ### **Optimize Page/Post URL** 

      Ex. **https://yoursite.com/your-primary-keyword**

      Include your primary keyword and use hyphens to separate words and don’t include unnecessary symbols or characters. You want the URL to be readable and understandable and not be too long or seem spammy. Keep it short, readable the entire thing should be less than 80 characters.  
    


- [ ] ### **Add internal links to your existing content (where relevant)** 

      Once this content piece is finished, review it for opportunities to link to any of your other existing pages or posts you have related to this topic (remember to check the “related pages” you listed in the overview table above). 

      Make sure **linked text** contains relevant keywords for the pages they go to. 

      Ex. “Colorado is an incredible place to elope whether you’re wanting to get married outside or at one of the many stunning **colorado mountain wedding venues**”

- [ ] ### **Add internal links from your existing content to this new content piece** 

      Once this new content piece is published, review your existing pages (*especially* your most powerful pages that are already ranking well and have the most backlinks) for opportunities  to link \*to\* this new page/post (remember to check the “related pages” you listed in the overview table above). 

      Make sure **linked text** contains relevant keywords to this new page.

      Ex. “Colorado is home to some of the best mountain wedding venues in the world and also the most stunning **elopement locations**.”  
        
        
      

- [ ] ### **Image Optimization**

      

      - [ ] Images are in google-search supported formats (JPEG, PNG, BMP, GIF, WebP, and SVG)

            - [ ] Image filenames have been optimized

                  *Google prefers images with short, descriptive filenames like colorado-mountain-elopement.jpg and suggests avoiding generic filenames like image1.jpg or non descriptive filenames like img00047.jpg  —  see google’s [Image SEO Best Practices](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/google-images) for more details.*

            - [ ] Images have been resized to the size they will be viewed on desktop 

                  *Tip: You can use this [Page Ruler Google Chrome extension](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/page-ruler/jcbmcnpepaddcedmjdcmhbekjhbfnlff?utm_source=ext_sidebar&hl=en-US) (free) to see viewable size. Then, add 10% buffer to that size (multiply the number of pixels on long edge pixels by 1.1) then resize to that many pixels long in Lightroom (or other program).*

            - [ ] Images have been optimized for load speed

                  *Tip: Use [Shortpixel’s compression tool](https://shortpixel.com/online-image-compression) (free for 50 images at a time) on the “glossy” setting for best balancecamme of image quality to file size reduction. Aim for less than \~100-150kb max for all images.*

            - [ ] Images have been placed next to relevant text and headings 

            - [ ] Alt-text has been added

                  *Alt-text should be very descriptive and accurately describe the image, don’t stuff with keywords as it's used for screen-reading tools for the visually impaired and by google for google image search so it should be accurate and true to the image content.*

      

- [ ] ### **Publish & Share** 

      The sooner your page or post can get traffic as soon as it’s published, the better. So publish it at a time when your audience is online and you can share it to social media straight away. 

- [ ] ### **Keep it Fresh & Updated** 

      It’s the best practice to update each of your posts once a year or when anything substantial has changed related to it to ensure it stays relevant, is still the highest quality & best answer to a searchers question, and has accurate up-to-date information.
